Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-30 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: Momentum-Ranker Dashboard Today's data reveals a stark contrast between the top two momentum candidates. CLMT dominates with a composite score of 94.5 and rank #1, driven by exceptional returns across all timeframes—160.78% over one year, 87.07% over six months, and 9.4% just today—while maintaining all four SMA gates (30/50/100/200 day). However, its momentum_signal of 2.87 triggers an "overextended" flag, indicating the move may have stretched beyond sustainable levels. NTR ranks second with more modest but steadier gains (51.18% one-year return) and crucially shows a bad_spy_score of only 0.1677 compared to CLMT's 0.9864, meaning NTR has proven far more resilient when SPY experiences down days. CLMT's ratio_passes score of 3 also lags NTR's 4, suggesting less consistency in momentum acceleration across timeframes. The data suggests a trade-off between maximum near-term gains (CLMT) and more balanced, market-resistant momentum (NTR).
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
